description: Sidebar & Top Toolbar Redesign (Frontend Phase 2) - Modern SaaS navigation with grouped sidebar, user profile block, desktop toolbar with search/help/notifications/user menu

userStories.title: Add i18n keys for sidebar and toolbar redesign
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all new UI strings added to both en.json and pt.json before building components that reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add nav group labels to both locale files: 'nav.groups.overview' (Overview/Visão Geral), 'nav.groups.collections' (Collections/Coleções), 'nav.groups.account' (Account/Conta)
Add 'app.tagline' key: 'Collection Manager' / 'Gerenciador de Coleções'
Add user menu keys: 'nav.userMenu.settings' (Settings/Configurações), 'nav.userMenu.help' (Help/Ajuda), 'nav.userMenu.profile' (Profile/Perfil), 'nav.userMenu.logout' (Logout/Sair)

Add toolbar keys: 'toolbar.search' (Search collections.../Buscar coleções...), 'toolbar.help' (Help/Ajuda), 'toolbar.helpComingSoon' (Coming soon/Em breve), 'toolbar.notifications' (Notifications/Notificações), 'toolbar.noNotifications' (No notifications/Sem notificações), 'toolbar.shortcut' for keyboard shortcut hint
No hardcoded strings — all new keys present in both en.json and pt.json
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must be done first since all subsequent UI stories reference these keys.

userStories.title: Add DropdownMenu shadcn/ui component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the DropdownMenu primitive from shadcn/ui available since the user profile block and toolbar user menu depend on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add DropdownMenu component to src/web/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx following shadcn/ui pattern with Radix primitives (@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu)
Export DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuLabel at minimum
Install @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu if not already installed
Component uses existing design tokens (bg-popover, text-popover-foreground, border, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check if dropdown-menu.tsx already exists in components/ui/ or components/ds/ first. The ds/dropdown-menu.tsx barrel export exists — verify if it wraps Radix or is something else.

userStories.title: Extract shared navigation group data structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the navigation items and groups defined as a shared data structure so both Sidebar and MobileSidebarContent can use it without duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update the navItems structure in sidebar.tsx to be grouped: { label: i18n key, items: NavItem[] } with three groups: Overview (Dashboard), Collections (Collections, Collection Types, Wishlist), Account (Profile)
Export the grouped nav structure so MobileSidebarContent can import it
Existing navItems flat array replaced with the grouped structure
No functional changes to navigation yet — just data restructuring
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a refactor step. Navigation should still render the same items, just sourced from the new grouped structure.

userStories.title: Rebuild sidebar header with brand identity block
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar header to prominently display the GeekVault logo and tagline so the app feels like a polished product.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded sidebar header shows vault icon + 'GeekVault' title text + 'Collection Manager' subtitle (using i18n key 'app.tagline')
Header height is 72px (h-[72px]) with proper vertical centering
Collapsed sidebar shows only the vault icon centered horizontally in 72px wide space
Title uses font-display (Nunito), subtitle uses smaller muted text
Logo area has consistent padding and no unbalanced empty space
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use existing logo assets: vaultIcon for collapsed, and vaultIcon + text for expanded (not logoFull image).

userStories.title: Update sidebar dimensions and spacing
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar to feel more spacious and premium with proper sizing.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded sidebar width changed from w-64 (256px) to w-[260px]
Collapsed sidebar width changed from w-16 (64px) to w-[72px]
Nav item height is 44px (min-h-[44px] or py-[10px] with text)
Icon size increased from h-4 w-4 (16px) to h-5 w-5 (20px) for all nav icons
Gap between navigation groups is 16px (gap-4)
Overall padding and spacing feels balanced — not cramped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Apply to both Sidebar and MobileSidebarContent.

userStories.title: Render navigation groups with labels in sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want sidebar navigation items grouped by category with visible group labels so I can quickly scan and find what I need.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation renders three groups: Overview (Dashboard), Collections (Collections, Collection Types, Wishlist), Account (Profile)
Group labels are small (text-xs), muted (text-sidebar-foreground/50), uppercase, and rendered above each group's items
Group labels are hidden when sidebar is collapsed
Gap of 16px between groups
In collapsed mode, groups are separated by subtle increased spacing or a thin horizontal line
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses the grouped nav structure from US-003 and i18n keys from US-001.

userStories.title: Improve active navigation item state
userStories.description: As a user, I want the active navigation item to be clearly highlighted so I always know where I am.
acceptanceCriteria: Active item has a soft accent background fill (bg-sidebar-accent)
Active item has a 3px left border highlight using sidebar-primary color (border-l-[3px] border-sidebar-primary)
Active item icon is colored with sidebar-primary when active (not muted)
Hover state is visually distinct from active state (lighter background, no left border)
Works correctly in both expanded and collapsed modes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Current implementation already has border-l-2 — increase to 3px and ensure icon color changes on active.

userStories.title: Fix collapsed sidebar dock behavior
userStories.description: As a user, I want the collapsed sidebar to behave like a dock — centered icons, proper tooltips, and clear active states.
acceptanceCriteria: Collapsed sidebar width is exactly 72px (w-[72px])
All nav icons are perfectly centered horizontally and vertically using flex justify-center items-center
Icon size is h-5 w-5 (20px) in collapsed mode
Every nav item shows a tooltip on hover (right side) with the page name
Active item in collapsed mode has the same visual treatment as expanded (background + left border + colored icon)
Navigation group separators visible as increased gaps between groups
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Tooltips already exist — verify they work correctly with the new sizing.

userStories.title: Remove bottom utility buttons and add sidebar collapse toggle to edge
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar bottom cleaned up (no logout/collapse buttons) and the collapse toggle moved to a hover-reveal chevron at the sidebar edge.
acceptanceCriteria: Logout button removed from sidebar bottom (both desktop Sidebar and MobileSidebarContent)
Collapse/expand chevron button removed from sidebar bottom
Small chevron button appears at the right edge of the sidebar, vertically centered
Chevron is hidden by default (opacity-0) and appears on hover over the sidebar (opacity-100 with transition)
Clicking chevron toggles sidebar between expanded (260px) and collapsed (72px)
Collapsed state persists in localStorage (existing STORAGE_KEY behavior preserved)
The toggle-sidebar custom event still works (for command palette integration)
Remove unused imports: LogOut, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight if only used for removed buttons (ChevronLeft/Right may be reused for edge toggle)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Logout moves to the user profile dropdown menu (US-010). Keep ChevronLeft/ChevronRight imports for the edge toggle.

userStories.title: Add user profile block with dropdown menu at sidebar bottom
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my avatar, name, and email at the bottom of the sidebar and click it to access account actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded sidebar bottom shows: default avatar icon (User icon from lucide or similar), display name, email — separated from nav by a top border
Clicking the user profile block opens a DropdownMenu with: Profile, Settings (disabled), Help (disabled), separator, Logout
Dropdown menu items have appropriate icons (User, Settings/Cog, HelpCircle, LogOut)
Profile navigates to /profile, Logout logs out and redirects to /login
Settings item is visually disabled (opacity-50, cursor-not-allowed, no click action)
Help item is visually disabled (opacity-50, cursor-not-allowed, no click action)
Collapsed sidebar shows only the avatar icon centered; clicking opens the same dropdown
Uses i18n keys from US-001 for all menu item labels
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extract a shared UserMenu component that can be reused in the top toolbar (US-013). Use DropdownMenu from US-002.

userStories.title: Create desktop top toolbar shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a persistent top toolbar on desktop that provides a consistent header area above the content.
acceptanceCriteria: New TopToolbar component created at src/web/src/components/layout/top-toolbar.tsx
Top toolbar is visible on desktop (hidden on mobile via md:flex, hidden below md)
Toolbar height is 72px (h-[72px]) matching the sidebar header for visual alignment
Toolbar has a bottom border using border-b border-border
Toolbar uses flex layout with items-center and horizontal padding
Layout in app-layout.tsx updated: toolbar renders above main content area, inside the flex-1 column next to the sidebar
Mobile header remains unchanged — only visible below md breakpoint
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is just the shell. Search, icons, and user menu are added in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Add search trigger to top toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a search input in the top toolbar that opens the command palette when clicked.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input-like button on the left side of the toolbar with placeholder text from i18n key 'toolbar.search'
Clicking the search trigger opens the existing command palette by dispatching a Ctrl+K / Cmd+K keyboard event or by using shared state
Search trigger shows the keyboard shortcut hint ('⌘K' on Mac, 'Ctrl+K' on other) as a subtle kbd badge inside the input
Styled as a subtle, rounded field with muted text and a search icon (Search from lucide) — not a full-width bar, roughly 250-300px wide
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The command palette already listens for Ctrl/Cmd+K. Consider programmatically dispatching the event or exposing a setter for the open state.

userStories.title: Add help, notifications, language toggle, and user menu to top toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want help, notifications, language toggle icons, and a user avatar menu on the right side of the toolbar.
acceptanceCriteria: Right side of toolbar shows (in order): Help icon, Notifications bell, Language toggle, User avatar — with consistent spacing
Help icon (HelpCircle from lucide) uses ghost button variant; clicking shows tooltip 'Coming soon' (i18n)
Notifications bell (Bell from lucide) uses ghost button variant; clicking shows a small popover/dropdown with 'No notifications' text (i18n)
Language toggle button (Languages from lucide) toggles between en/pt, same as current mobile header behavior

User avatar at far right shows initials circle or default avatar icon; clicking opens DropdownMenu with: user name + email label, separator, Profile, Settings (disabled), Help (disabled), separator, Logout
Reuse or share the UserMenu dropdown logic from the sidebar user profile block (US-010)
Theme toggle (Sun/Moon) also present as a toolbar icon, or integrated into user menu — must remain accessible on desktop
All buttons use consistent sizing (size='icon' or equivalent)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Move theme/language toggle from mobile-only header to the desktop toolbar. The layout order should be: Help | Notifications | Language | Theme | User.

userStories.title: Update mobile sidebar content to match new design
userStories.description: As a user on mobile, I want the sheet-based sidebar to reflect the same grouped navigation and user profile block as the desktop sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: MobileSidebarContent updated with the same navigation groups (Overview, Collections, Account) with group labels
User profile block at bottom with avatar, display name, email
Tapping user profile block shows the same DropdownMenu (Profile, Settings disabled, Help disabled, Logout)
Standalone logout button removed — only accessible via user profile dropdown
Mobile header remains unchanged (hamburger + logo + theme/language toggles)
Navigation items use the shared grouped nav data structure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse the shared nav groups and UserMenu component.

userStories.title: Update existing tests for sidebar redesign
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing sidebar and header tests updated so the test suite passes with the new layout structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing sidebar tests updated to reflect grouped navigation structure (group labels rendered)
Tests no longer assert on removed elements (standalone logout button, collapse chevron at bottom)
Tests verify user profile block renders with display name at sidebar bottom
Existing header tests still pass (mobile header unchanged in behavior)
All tests pass: npm test exits cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Focus on updating broken assertions. Don't add new component tests here — that's US-016.

userStories.title: Add tests for new TopToolbar and UserMenu components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the new components to maintain test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: TopToolbar component has a co-located test file (top-toolbar.test.tsx) in components/layout/
Test verifies toolbar renders search trigger, help icon, notifications icon, language toggle, and user avatar
Test verifies search trigger interaction (opens command palette or dispatches event)
UserMenu component (if extracted as shared) has a co-located test file
Test verifies UserMenu shows Profile, Settings, Help, Logout items
Test verifies Logout calls logout function and navigates to /login
All tests pass: npm test exits cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing test patterns in the codebase (vitest + testing-library). Check test-utils.tsx for render helpers.
